farmer_report.agronomist_summary: Bacterial leaf blight is a significant disease of rice, causing substantial yield loss and grain quality degradation. Early detection and prompt management are crucial to mitigate its impact.

Water-soaked or necrotic areas on the leaves

farmer_report.diagnosis: Yellowing of panicles, followed by a watery exudate and a characteristic 'blight' appearance.

Apply copper-based bactericides as a foliar spray
prevention_tips: Maintain good crop hygiene
Use resistant cultivars

visual_feature_analysis: Yellowing or stunting of plants
Leaf distortion or malformation

pathogen_profile.scientific_name: Rice tungro spherical virus (RTSV) and Babesia oryzae
pathogen_profile.infection_mechanism: RTSV: Systemic infection through insect vectors; B. oryzae: Systemic infection through insect vectors
pathogen_profile.disease_cycle_stage: RTSV: Persistent in insect vectors; B. oryzae: Persistent in insect vectors

scientist_report.environmental_correlation: Temperature (25-35°C), humidity (60-80%), and rainfall (500-1000 mm) influence disease severity
scientist_report.epidemiological_risk: High risk of disease spread through insect vectors and contaminated water
scientist_report.research_recommendation: Further studies on the molecular mechanisms of RTSV and B. oryzae infection, and the development of resistant rice varieties
